4	Date: 2026-06-04

8	Coupled docs: plan-2026-06-03-helix-library-FINAL.md, implementation-plan-2026-06-04-helix-library-family.md, test-plan-2026-06-04-helix-library-family.md.

11	
12	## §0 Architecture amendment (relative to design-2026-06-03-helix-library-split.md)
13	
14	### §0.1 Terminology
15	
16	This document uses the v2 family terminology: a HELIX flow is what was called a methodology in v1. The marker key `flows:` is canonical; `methodologies:` is accepted via the M020 deprecation alias for one cycle. CLI flag `--flow` is canonical; `--methodology` is a one-cycle alias. Env var `HELIX_FLOW` supersedes `HELIX_METHODOLOGY` (still accepted). See plan-2026-06-05-conversation-bench-and-autonomy.md §11 for the full rename mapping.
17	
18	Two architectural changes land on top of the library/flow split:
19	
20	1. **Activation moves from heuristics to explicit declaration via a marker
21	   file `.helix.yml` at the repo root.** Today's detection signals
22	   (`workflows/methodology.yml` path, file globs, prompt patterns, the
23	   §7.5 precedence ladder with alphabetical tie-break) are FROZEN at
24	   today's behaviour and demoted to a fallback path that fires only when
25	   `.helix.yml` is absent. With a marker present, activation is
26	   deterministic and per-repo.
27	
28	2. **Inter-artifact linkages are split three ways.** Today, `meta.yml`
29	   carries `relationships: {depends_on, informs, referenced_by}` at the
30	   TYPE level, mixing flow-graph information with portable
31	   artifact shape. Under the relaxation:
32	
33	   - **Library `meta.yml`** carries SHAPE ONLY (`required_sections`,
34	     `prompts`, `template`, `quality_checks`, `section_aliases`). No
35	     `relationships:`, no `can_link_to:`, no edge information of any
36	     kind. The Phase 1 inventory confirms this is already the de-facto
37	     pattern (most types have empty `relationships:`; helix-infra types
38	     declare none at all).
39	   - **Flow `graph.yml`** declares which TYPE-PAIR edges are
40	     allowed in this flow, with edge kind and per-pair
41	     cardinality / strength. The flow's outbound
42	     cross-flow edges live in a sibling `external_edges:` /
43	     `cross_flow:` block in the same file.
44	   - **Instance frontmatter** (under `ddx.links:`) declares the specific
45	     edges THIS instance has, referencing siblings by stable id (never
46	     file path). The validator resolves edges against the active
47	     flow graph(s), which are named by the marker.
48	
49	The two changes are coupled: the marker file is the input that tells the
50	validator (and the skill) WHICH flow graphs to load and validate
51	instance edges against. Cross-flow edges (e.g. a product PRD that
52	informs an infra change-intent) work ONLY when both flows appear
53	in the active marker and the source flow has authorized the edge
54	in its `external_edges:` block.
55	
56	Everything from design-2026-06-03 §0–§5 (monorepo topology, library
57	contents, `meta.yml` shape, flow skeleton, library type
58	versioning) stays. §6 (graph) is extended (additional edge kinds,
59	`external_edges:`). §7.5 (co-activation precedence) is partly
60	superseded: rules 1, 2, 5 survive as fallback; rules 3, 4 are subsumed
61	by the marker. §8 conflicts already resolved stay resolved.
62	
63	---
64	
65	## §1 Marker file
66	
67	### 1.1 Location and discovery
68	
69	`.helix.yml` at the repository root — the directory containing `.git/`,
70	or the cwd if no git root is found. Single file, no nested overrides in
71	v1. The dotfile-at-root convention matches `.editorconfig`, `.gitignore`,
72	`.prettierrc`. The name `helix` is the FAMILY name (marketplace), not a
73	flow id; the same file declares which flow flavors apply.
74	
75	`.claude-plugin/methodologies.yml` was considered and rejected: it
76	couples the file to one agent harness. TOML was considered and rejected:
77	the rest of the family (graph.yml, meta.yml, methodology.yml) is YAML.
78	
79	**Discovery walk + stray-marker rule.** The validator walks upward from
80	cwd to the git root searching for `.helix.yml`. The FIRST one found is
81	the active marker. After resolution the validator scans the whole tree
82	under the git root for additional `.helix.yml` files; every stray
83	marker (any `.helix.yml` other than the active one) emits warning
84	**M010** ("nested .helix.yml found; v1 ignores nested markers, parent:
85	semantics reserved for v2"). v1 ignores them; v2 may flip M010 to error
86	or use the reserved `parent:` field to implement nested overrides.
87	
88	A separate `helix_check.py marker --discover` mode runs ONLY the tree
89	scan (no activation) for one-shot linting: it prints every `.helix.yml`
90	under cwd with its relative path and an exit code of 0 if exactly one
91	marker is found, otherwise non-zero. CI invokes `--discover` to catch
92	stray markers that the activation walk would warn about silently.
93	
94	### 1.2 Schema
95	
96	One required top-level key `flows:` (list). Optional top-level
97	keys `helix_version:`, `defaults:`, `cross_flow_edges:`. Optional
98	per-entry keys `version:`, `concerns:`.
99	
100	```yaml
101	# .helix.yml — schema (informal; JSON Schema at library/schemas/marker.schema.json)
102	helix_version: 1                       # marker schema version (REQUIRED in v1)
103	
104	flows:                         # REQUIRED; list of {id, root, ...}
105	  - id: <plugin-id>                    # must match methodology.yml `id:`
106	    root: <repo-relative-path>         # where instances live; must stay inside repo
107	    version: "<semver>"                # OPTIONAL; advisory pin
108	    concerns:                          # OPTIONAL; couples with concerns slot model
109	      enabled:  [<concern-slug>, ...]
110	      disabled: [<concern-slug>, ...]
111	
112	defaults:                              # OPTIONAL; resolves generic prompts
113	  flow: <id>                    # must be one of the listed flows
114	
115	cross_flow_edges:               # OPTIONAL; per-repo allowlist
116	  allow:
117	    - from: <src-flow>:<src-type>
118	      to:   <dst-flow>:<dst-type>
119	      kind: <edge-kind>
120	```
121	
122	### 1.3 Fallback when marker is absent
123	
124	A missing `.helix.yml` does NOT block activation. The skill falls
125	through to the frozen-today heuristics (the §7.5 detection block in the
126	prior design) and emits ONE banner on first activation per session:
127	
128	```
129	No .helix.yml found. Activating <id> by heuristic (path: workflows/methodology.yml).
130	Run /helix init-marker to make this explicit.
131	```
132	
133	Heuristic activation is FROZEN — no new detection signals get added; the
134	marker is the growth path. Refusing to activate would punish every
135	existing HELIX install. Silently activating the first flow found
136	hides drift. The banner is the explicit middle path.
137	
138	### 1.4 Hard-fail rules (no partial activation on malformed marker)
139	
140	A marker that exists must be valid or activation stops. Asymmetry is
141	intentional: the marker exists precisely to eliminate silent
142	misroutes; soft-failing it would reintroduce the failure mode it
143	prevents.
144	
145	- **YAML parse error** → hard stop (M001), print file + line + parse message.
146	- **`root:` escapes the repo root** → hard stop (M002).
147	- **Duplicate `id:` in `flows:`** → hard stop (M003).
148	- **`defaults.flow:` not in `flows:`** → hard stop (M004).
149	- **Unknown `id:` (no installed plugin matches)** → that entry is
150	  ignored with diagnostic M005; OTHER entries proceed. Rationale: a
151	  missing plugin is recoverable (`install the plugin`) and shouldn't
152	  black-hole the entire repo.
153	- **`root:` path resolves to a directory that does not exist on disk
154	  (or exists but is not a directory)** → hard stop M006, naming the
155	  offending entry and the unresolved path. The marker is supposed to
156	  eliminate typos in scope declarations; silently treating a missing
157	  scope as "zero instances" reintroduces the false-green failure mode.
158	  Escape hatch: `--allow-empty-scope` demotes M006 to a warning so a
159	  fresh repo can declare `root: docs/helix/` before any instances
160	  exist. CI runs without that flag and catches dead scopes.
161	
162	### 1.5 Resolution of "which flow is active right now"
163	
164	When the marker lists 2+ flows, the skill chooses one per
165	invocation:
166	
167	1. Explicit `/<flow-id> <verb>` prefix wins.
168	2. `HELIX_FLOW=<id>` env var wins.
169	3. cwd under one flow's `root:` wins.
170	4. `defaults.flow:` in the marker wins.
171	5. If exactly one flow is listed, it wins.
172	6. Otherwise emit the disambiguation banner from the prior §7.5 (single
173	   line; deterministic tie-break by listed order, NOT alphabetical —
174	   the marker IS the operator's intent).
175	
176	### 1.6 Worked examples
177	
178	**Product-only repo (common case).**
179	
180	```yaml
181	helix_version: 1
182	flows:
183	  - id: helix
184	    root: docs/helix/
185	```
186	
187	**Mixed monorepo (product + infra).**
188	
189	```yaml
190	helix_version: 1
191	
192	flows:
193	  - id: helix
194	    root: docs/helix/
195	    version: "1.0.0"
196	    concerns:
197	      enabled:  [accessibility, verification, measurement]
198	      disabled: [sample-data]
199	  - id: helix-infra
200	    root: infra/terraform/
201	    version: "0.2.0"
202	
203	defaults:
204	  flow: helix
205	
206	cross_flow_edges:
207	  allow:
208	    - from: helix:prd
209	      to:   helix-infra:change-intent
210	      kind: informs
211	```
212	
213	**Malformed (unknown flow id).**
214	
215	```yaml
216	helix_version: 1
217	flows:
218	  - id: helix
219	    root: docs/helix/
220	  - id: helix-mobile          # not installed
221	    root: docs/mobile/
222	```
223	
224	Expected diagnostic (entry ignored, others proceed):
225	
226	```
227	.helix.yml: unknown flow id 'helix-mobile' at line 5.
228	Installed flows: [helix, helix-infra]
229	This entry is ignored. Other entries (helix) will activate.
230	To install: see https://<family-marketplace>/plugins
231	```
232	
233	**Malformed (root escapes repo) — hard stop.**
234	
235	```
236	.helix.yml: root path '../../shared/helix/' for flow 'helix'
237	escapes the repo root (.git is at /repos/acme/). Refusing to activate.
238	Fix: use a repo-relative path that stays inside this repo.
239	```
240	
241	---
242	
243	## §2 Linkage relaxation (type / flow / instance cut)
244	
245	### 2.1 Layer 1 — Library type `meta.yml`: SHAPE ONLY
246	
247	Removing `relationships:` from library `meta.yml` is a structural rule
248	the type validator enforces. A library type is portable across
249	flows; encoding edges at the type level re-couples it to one
250	flow's vocabulary.
251	
252	```yaml
253	# library/types/prd/meta.yml
254	id: prd
255	name: Product Requirements Document
256	summary: |
257	  Captures the WHAT and WHY of a product change. Flow-agnostic shape.
258	
259	required_sections:
260	  - summary
261	  - problem
262	  - users
263	  - functional_requirements
264	  - non_functional_requirements
265	  - success_metrics
266	  - out_of_scope
267	
268	section_aliases:
269	  functional_requirements: [functional-requirements, frs, fr]
270	  non_functional_requirements: [non-functional-requirements, nfrs, nfr]
271	
272	quality_checks:
273	  - id: success_metrics_measurable
274	    description: Each success metric has a measurable target.
275	    severity: blocking
276	
277	prompts:
278	  generation: prompt.md
279	  review:     review.md
280	
281	template:
282	  file: template.md
283	
284	tags: [framing, requirements]
285	version: 1.0.0
286	# INVARIANT: no `relationships:`, no `can_link_to:`, no edge information of any kind.
287	```
288	
289	The type validator (§4) hard-fails on any `meta.yml` that carries a
290	`relationships:` key under exit code class 3 (T-class).
291	
292	**Library type version semantics (semver).** The `version:` on a
293	library type is semver-tracked. The validator interprets shape changes:
294	
295	- **Adding** a `required_sections` entry is a MAJOR bump.
296	- **Renaming** a section without an alias is a MAJOR bump.
297	- **Adding** an optional/aliased section, tightening a `quality_check`
298	  severity, adding a `section_aliases` entry are MINOR bumps.
299	- **Documentation / `summary` / `tags` / prompt prose** edits are PATCH bumps.
300	
301	The library publish gate (T-mode validator) emits **T010** if a
302	shape-affecting change ships without bumping `version:` accordingly.
303	
304	**Instance-shape resolution against version.** Type-shape checks
305	validate against the currently-resolved library version (the version
306	on disk now), NOT against the instance's advisory `library_type_version:`
307	pin. BUT the instance pin gates whether NEW constraints fire as ERROR
308	vs DEPRECATION-WARNING:
309	
310	- If `library_type_version:` declared on the instance has the SAME
311	  major as the currently-resolved library version → constraints fire
312	  as errors (normal mode).
313	- If `library_type_version:` has a LOWER major than the currently-
314	  resolved version → NEWLY-introduced required sections fire as
315	  **I010 deprecation warning** for one major-version cycle, citing
316	  the prior-major pin and the major bump that introduced the new
317	  section. The hook does not block; CI in --strict mode upgrades
318	  I010 to error.
319	- If the instance has no `library_type_version:` field → treat as
320	  matching current major (no grace period). Authors who want grace
321	  must opt in by pinning.
322	
323	This closes the verification-exit-gate failure mode where a library
324	update silently breaks pre-commit on every uncommitted ADR: a major
325	bump gives in-flight instances one cycle to migrate.
326	
327	### 2.2 Layer 2 — Flow `graph.yml`: TYPE-PAIR allowed edges
328	
329	The graph declares which type-pair edges are allowed and at what
330	strength. Edge kinds are CLOSED at five values:
331	
332	| Kind          | Semantics                                                                                  | Acyclicity walk? |
333	| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------- |
334	| `requires`    | Hard prerequisite. Target must exist before source is dispatchable.                        | Yes              |
335	| `informs`     | Forward soft edge; traceability only.                                                      | No               |
336	| `contains`    | Parent → child decomposition (PRD contains FR; FEAT contains user-story).                  | Yes              |
337	| `supersedes`  | Replacement edge. Type-pair declares "this type may supersede instances of that type."     | No (instance-only chain) |
338	| `may_surface` | Optional production from a node's working session.                                         | No               |
339	
340	The inventory's `referenced_by` and `informed_by` are DROPPED at this
341	layer — they were inverse views of `informs` / `requires` and were the
342	source of the "PRD informs test-plan vs test-plan referenced_by PRD"
343	double-encoding bug Phase 1 caught. The validator computes inverse
344	views from forward edges on demand.
345	
346	Extensibility: edge kinds outside the five are accepted as
347	`x-<vendor>-<kind>` (e.g. `x-team-blocks`) and the validator ignores
348	them. Renderers may surface them. Promoting an `x-` kind to a closed
349	kind costs a validator contract bump (§4).
350	
351	**Cardinality and strength live on the type-pair edge in the
352	flow graph, not on the type and not in instance frontmatter.**
353	The graph row carries `required: true|false` (default `false`) and
354	optional `cardinality: one-to-one|one-to-many|many-to-many`. The same
355	type pair can appear in different activities with different strengths.
356	
357	**Cross-flow edges are declared by the SOURCE flow only,
358	in an `external_edges:` block in the same `graph.yml`.** The target
359	flow does not declare anything. Rationale: a separate bridge
360	file adds a third linkage location and creates the "who owns the
361	bridge?" drift mode the inventory already shows for un-graphed
362	flows. A bilateral declaration doubles the maintenance surface
363	for the advisory `informs` cases that are the only cross-flow
364	edges anyone has asked for. If a future flow needs ENFORCED
365	cross-flow `requires`, that's a separate amendment.
366	
367	**`external_edges[]` entries MUST NOT carry `required: true`.** The
368	graph validator hard-fails (**G104**) any external edge with
369	`required: true`. Rationale: enforced cross-flow prerequisites
370	are the deferred bilateral-mechanism case (§6.2); permitting
371	`required: true` on an external edge would let a downstream
372	flow silently invalidate every existing source-flow
373	instance the moment the edge gets added to the graph. With external
374	edges always advisory, adding a new external edge never invalidates
375	existing instances retroactively — only new instances feel the
376	optional `informs` traceability slot.
377	
378	Additionally, **`external_edges[].kind:` MUST be `informs` or an
379	`x-`-namespaced kind** (G105). `requires` / `contains` /
380	`supersedes` are forbidden across flows in v1; they imply
381	enforcement or instance lifecycle the bilateral mechanism is needed
382	to safely express.
383	
384	```yaml
385	# product/workflows/graph.yml — fragment
386	version: 1
387	flow:
388	  id: helix
389	  library_version: "^1.0.0"
390	  validator_contract: 1
391	
392	activities:
393	  - { id: 00-discover, exit_gate: discover-validation }
394	  - { id: 01-frame,    exit_gate: prd-validation }
395	  - { id: 02-design,   exit_gate: design-validation }
396	  - { id: 03-test,     exit_gate: test-validation }
397	  - { id: 04-build,    exit_gate: build-validation }
398	  - { id: 06-iterate,  exit_gate: iteration-validation }
399	
400	nodes:
401	  - id: product-vision
402	    type: library:product-vision
403	    activity: 00-discover
404	    cardinality: singleton
405	    role: anchor
406	  - id: prd
407	    type: library:prd
408	    activity: 01-frame
409	    cardinality: many
410	  - id: feature-specification
411	    type: library:feature-specification
412	    activity: 01-frame
413	    cardinality: many
414	  - id: user-story
415	    type: library:user-stories
416	    activity: 01-frame
417	    cardinality: many
418	  - id: adr
419	    type: library:adr
420	    activity: 02-design
421	    cardinality: many
422	    scope: cross-cutting
423	  - id: technical-design
424	    type: library:technical-design
425	    activity: 02-design
426	    cardinality: many
427	  - id: test-plan
428	    type: library:test-plan
429	    activity: 03-test
430	    cardinality: many
431	  - id: implementation-plan
432	    type: library:implementation-plan
433	    activity: 04-build
434	    cardinality: many
435	
436	edges:
437	  - { from: product-vision,        to: prd,                   kind: informs,    required: true  }
438	  - { from: prd,                   to: feature-specification, kind: informs,    required: true  }
439	  - { from: prd,                   to: principles,            kind: informs,    required: false }
440	  - { from: feature-specification, to: user-story,            kind: contains,   required: true  }
441	  - { from: feature-specification, to: technical-design,      kind: informs,    required: false }
442	  - { from: prd,                   to: test-plan,             kind: informs,    required: true  }
443	  - { from: adr,                   to: technical-design,      kind: informs,    required: false }
444	  - { from: technical-design,      to: implementation-plan,   kind: informs,    required: true  }
445	  - { from: test-plan,             to: implementation-plan,   kind: informs,    required: true  }
446	  - { from: adr,                   to: adr,                   kind: supersedes, required: false }
447	
448	allowed_cycles:
449	  - from_type: implementation-plan
450	    to_type:   prd
451	    kind:      informs
452	    rationale: |
453	      06-iterate learnings re-open 01-frame. Each pass is a new walk;
454	      superseding PRD instances carry `supersedes: [PRD-003]` in frontmatter.
455	
456	external_edges:
457	  - to_flow: helix-infra
458	    from_type:      prd
459	    to_type:        change-intent
460	    kind:           informs
461	    cardinality:    one-to-many
462	    required:       false
463	    rationale: |
464	      A product PRD may request infrastructure work that lands as a
465	      change-intent in the active infra scope. The infra graph does
466	      not mirror this edge — informs is advisory-forward.
467	```
468	
469	### 2.3 Layer 3 — Instance frontmatter: ACTUAL edges by id
470	
471	Edges in instance frontmatter live under `ddx.links:` as a LIST (same
472	target may appear with different kinds). Targets are id strings
473	resolved against the active flows' instance indexes. Path-based
474	references are rejected — they bake repo layout into documents.
475	
476	Existing `ddx.id` and `ddx.review` fields are untouched and
477	runtime-managed. The relaxation is ADDITIVE: a document without
478	`ddx.links:` validates with a WARNING for the first deprecation cycle
479	(traceability degraded, shape still enforced). A migration script (§5)
480	proposes `ddx.links:` entries from existing body prose (FEAT/ADR
481	"Related" cells, `depends_on` lists already in some frontmatter, paired-with
482	prose in helix-infra).
483	
484	```yaml
485	---
486	ddx:
487	  id: PRD-001
488	  type: prd                              # library type id
489	  flow: helix                     # flow that owns this instance
490	  library_type_version: 1.0.0            # advisory; pairs with marker version
491	
492	  review:                                # runtime-managed; unchanged
493	    self_hash: 2b22383538b33c6ecee57f43d85128dfef7d56254766b757aa36439e35f2bfc9
494	    deps: {}
495	    reviewed_at: "2026-05-24T23:26:16Z"
496	
497	  links:                                 # author-managed; new
498	    - { kind: informs,   to: FEAT-001 }
499	    - { kind: informs,   to: FEAT-002 }
500	    - { kind: contains,  to: FR-1, scope: intra-document }
501	    - { kind: supersedes, to: PRD-001@v0 }
502	    - { kind: informs,   to: "helix-infra:CI-2026-06-runtime-boundary",
503	        cross_flow: true }
504	---
505	```
506	
507	Field rules:
508	
509	- `kind:` must be one of the five closed kinds OR an `x-` namespaced
510	  string (warning, not error).
511	- `to:` is an id. For local edges, an id resolvable in this
512	  flow's instance index. For cross-flow edges, a
513	  qualified id `<flow-id>:<instance-id>` and
514	  `cross_flow: true`.
515	- `scope: intra-document` marks containment edges to in-body anchors
516	  (FR-n, US-n-ACm); these are NOT resolved against the cross-document
517	  index. They exist in frontmatter so traceability tooling can render
518	  PRD→FR→US chains without reparsing prose.
519	- `supersedes:` targets MAY use the `@v<n>` suffix when the prior
520	  generation has been archived; instance check warns if the target
521	  isn't marked superseded.
522	- `status:` on an individual edge entry is one of `present` (default)
523	  or `planned`. `planned` is the typed escape hatch for forward
524	  references to unauthored docs: the iterative-design case where the
525	  PRD is authored before its FEATs exist. `status: planned` downgrades
526	  the unresolved-target error **I101** to warning **I103** (forward
527	  reference unresolved) outside of exit-gate checks. At exit-gate
528	  time for the source activity, a `status: planned` entry that STILL
529	  fails to resolve is upgraded back to error I101 — the planned slot
530	  is for in-flight work, not permanent escape. A `status: planned`
531	  entry that DOES resolve to an existing id is itself an error
532	  (**I104** "use status: present once the target exists") so authors
533	  don't leave the placeholder marker after the target is authored.
534	  The I101 diagnostic mentions both the nearest-id hint AND the
535	  `status: planned` option, so authors can distinguish typo vs. forward
536	  reference at the point of failure.
537	
538	### 2.4 Resolution contract (binds all three layers)
539	
540	The validator (§4) loads the marker, builds a per-flow
541	`instance_index: {ddx.id → file_path}` by walking each flow's
542	`root:`, then for each `ddx.links[]` entry:
543	
544	1. Look up `(source_type, kind, target_type)` in the active
545	   flow's `edges:` (or `external_edges:` if
546	   `cross_flow: true`). If absent → error class I/G.
547	2. If the edge is in `external_edges:`, distinguish two unreachable
548	   modes:
549	   - **2a** target flow absent from the active marker but its
550	     plugin IS installed on disk → WARNING **I120** ("link to inactive
551	     flow; add `<id>:` to .helix.yml or remove the edge").
552	   - **2b** target flow plugin NOT installed on disk → WARNING
553	     **I121** ("link to uninstalled flow; install
554	     `<plugin-id>` or remove the edge"). Distinct diagnostics matter
555	     because the operator's next step differs.
556	   Both downgradable-by-default to error via
557	   `--cross-methodology-edges deny` or `--strict-cross-method`. At
558	   graph-load time the active flow's `external_edges:` are
559	   themselves walked; any entry whose target flow is absent
560	   from the marker emits **G140** once per graph load (not per
561	   instance edge that uses it), to surface graph drift even before
562	   any instance references it.
563	3. Resolve `to:` against the appropriate instance index. Unresolved →
564	   error I101 (unless `scope: intra-document` or
565	   `status: planned` — see §2.3).
566	4. Apply per-edge `required: true|false` against source-node
567	   cardinality — at-least-one-required edges generate errors only at
568	   activity-exit-gate time (a fresh PRD doesn't fail just for being new).
569	   `required: true` is NEVER honored on `external_edges:` (rejected at
570	   graph-load time by G104 in §2.2); cross-flow edges therefore
571	   never invalidate existing instances retroactively when added.
572	
573	### 2.5 Frontmatter write contract
574	
575	The skill MUST round-trip instance frontmatter through a
576	key-preserving emitter so that incidental edits never silently rewrite
577	shape. Specifically:
578	
579	1. **Preserve unknown keys verbatim.** Use stdlib `yaml.safe_load`
580	   into an `OrderedDict` (or insertion-ordered dict, Python ≥ 3.7),
581	   then `yaml.safe_dump(..., sort_keys=False, allow_unicode=True)` so
582	   round-trip preserves key order. Unknown top-level keys
583	   (`depends_on:`, `relationships:`, vendor-namespaced `x-*:`) MUST
584	   survive the round-trip byte-equivalent (modulo trailing whitespace
585	   normalization).
586	2. **Legacy → new key translation is migration-script-only.** The
587	   skill never translates legacy `depends_on:` or `relationships:`
588	   into `ddx.links:` on incidental edits. Translation is performed
589	   ONLY by the explicit migration script in §5.4. Determinism rule:
590	   two `/helix` agent runs on the same legacy-frontmatter PRD must
591	   produce byte-equivalent frontmatter.
592	3. **Validator surfaces coexistence.** When both legacy and new keys
593	   are present on the same instance, the validator emits warning
594	   **W005** ("legacy + ddx.links coexist; run
595	   `library/scripts/migrate_relationships_to_links.py` to consolidate")
596	   pointing at the migrate command. The hook does not block on W005.
597	4. **No drop on rewrite.** A skill that strips a key it does not
598	   understand on rewrite is a contract violation tested by T32.
599	
600	### 2.6 Drop list (what the relaxation removes)
601	
602	- `relationships:` block from library `meta.yml` (Layer 1).
603	- `referenced_by:` / `informed_by:` from flow `graph.yml`
604	  (computed inverse views).
605	- Path-based markdown links as canonical edges (still allowed as prose
606	  references; no longer the traceability source of truth).
607	- Implicit precedence rules 3 and 4 from prior §7.5 (subsumed by
608	  marker + §1.5 resolution chain).
609	
610	### 2.7 Edge Authority Asymmetry (Invariant 1)
611	
612	The three layers do not merely partition shape from edges from instances —
613	they assign distinct **authorities** to each layer, and the skill is the
614	deliberator between them.
615	
616	- **Layer 1 (library `meta.yml`)** declares the type's intrinsic shape.
617	  It has NO authority over edges. Whether two types may relate is decided
618	  one layer up.
619	- **Layer 2 (flow `graph.yml`)** declares what edges are
620	  *possible* — the type-pair-with-kind whitelist (cf. §2.2). An edge
621	  appearing in `graph.yml` is a **candidate**, not an obligation.
622	- **Layer 3 (instance frontmatter `ddx.links`)** declares what edges are
623	  *actual* for a specific document. Every instance edge is a deliberate
624	  authoring decision.
625	
626	**The skill MUST NOT mechanically populate `ddx.links` from `graph.yml`
627	edges.** Auto-populating instance edges from type-pair candidates — at
628	any autonomy level, including `autonomous` and `aggressive` — is a
629	contract violation. The graph enumerates what *could* be linked; the
630	skill's job is to surface those candidates to the operator (or, when the
631	operator has clearly named both endpoints, to confirm the link is
632	intentional) and only then write the edge into `ddx.links`.
633	
634	**Why this asymmetry holds.** A graph edge `prd informs feature-specification`
635	declares that *some* PRDs *may* inform *some* feature-specifications. It
636	does NOT declare that the PRD being authored right now informs FEAT-001,
637	FEAT-002, or any particular FEAT instance. That decision is content —
638	it belongs to the human author. The skill that infers "graph says
639	informs, so I'll wire ddx.links to every FEAT" turns a possibility
640	catalog into a mechanical join and erodes the traceability signal the
641	graph is supposed to certify.
642	
643	**Autonomy does not relax this.** `autonomy=autonomous` excuses the skill
644	from confirming each *in-scope mechanical write* (e.g., creating the PRD
645	file under the marker's root). It does NOT excuse skipping the
646	deliberation that turns a graph candidate into an instance edge —
647	deliberation is the asymmetry. Under `autonomous`, the skill must still
648	surface candidate edges and ask before populating `ddx.links`. The
649	`stop_at` set therefore implicitly includes "writing an `ddx.links` entry
650	not previously named by the operator" at every autonomy level.
651	
652	**How the bench enforces.** The conversation bench category
653	`edge-asymmetry` (plan §1.5b, rows EA-01..EA-04) supplies a workspace
654	with existing FEAT-001 and FEAT-002, a graph declaring
655	`prd informs feature-specification` (required:false), and the prompt
656	"Create a PRD". The runner asserts the agent surfaces FEAT-001 / FEAT-002
657	as candidate informs targets and asks before writing `ddx.links`. Rows
658	EA-01/EA-02 run under `autonomy=guided`; EA-03/EA-04 run under
659	`autonomy=autonomous`. PASS in both autonomy levels requires the same
660	deliberation prose. A failure mode where the skill writes
661	`ddx.links: [FEAT-001, FEAT-002]` silently — even when authoring the PRD
662	file is in-scope — fails the row and halts the bench (P4 halt
663	condition).
664

666	
667	## §3 graph.yml format + worked example
668	
669	### 3.1 On-disk format and validation contract
670	
671	`graph.yml` is hand-authored YAML. A JSON Schema
672	`library/schemas/graph.schema.json` accompanies it at
673	`validator_contract: 1`. The validator parses YAML, validates against
674	the schema for structural shape, then runs the four semantic checks
675	JSON Schema cannot express:
676	
677	1. **Type resolution** — every `nodes[].type` resolves: `library:<slug>`
678	   exists in the pinned library catalog; `local:<slug>` exists under
679	   `workflows/artifacts/<slug>/meta.yml`.
680	2. **Edge endpoint resolution** — every `edges[].from/to` references a
681	   declared node id; every `external_edges[].from_type` is a local
682	   node; every `external_edges[].to_type` is plausible against the
683	   target flow IF the target flow is loaded.
684	3. **Acyclicity over `requires` + `contains`** modulo `allowed_cycles`.
685	   **Self-loop rule (per S5 review item):** a type-pair edge with
686	   `from_type == to_type` on a kind in the acyclicity walk
687	   (`requires`, `contains`) is treated as a one-node cycle. It
688	   requires an explicit `allowed_cycles` entry for that
689	   (from_type, to_type, kind) triple; without one the validator
690	   emits **G103** ("same-type self-loop on walked kind requires
691	   allowed_cycles entry"). Self-loops on non-walked kinds
692	   (`informs`, `supersedes`, `may_surface`) pass without an
693	   `allowed_cycles` entry, but the schema-level check emits a single
694	   **G133** info-level note per graph listing them so they cannot
695	   accidentally proliferate.
696	4. **Exit-gate role** — every `activities[].exit_gate` references a
697	   node with `role: exit-gate`.
698	
699	### 3.2 Edge-kind reconciliation with prior §6.1.1
700	
701	The prior design declared three kinds: `requires`, `informs`,
702	`may_surface`. This design EXTENDS to five by adding `contains` (PRD →
703	FR-n; FEAT → user-story) and `supersedes` (ADR → prior ADR; PRD → prior
704	PRD). Rationale: the Phase 1 inventory found both shapes in the wild
705	already (containment by ID prefix, supersession in ADR status tables);
706	typed slots let instance frontmatter declare them rather than burying
707	them in prose.
708	
709	### 3.3 Strength annotation
710	
711	Per-edge `required: true|false` (default `false`) on the type-pair edge
712	declares whether instances of the source type MUST emit at least one
713	edge of this kind to the target type. The validator translates
714	`required: true` into an exit-gate-time check; it does NOT block
715	authoring of a fresh node. This separates "this flow demands
716	traceability" (graph) from "this PRD happens to inform two FEATs"
717	(instance).
718	
719	`min_outgoing:` / `min_incoming:` on a node are sugar for combined
720	cardinality across multiple type pairs (e.g. "every FEAT must trace
721	upward to a PRD via informs" → `min_incoming: { informs: { prd: 1 } }`).
722	Either spelling is accepted; `required: true` on the edge is preferred
723	for single-pair constraints.
724	
725	### 3.4 `allowed_cycles` — per type-pair-with-kind, with rationale
726	
727	The HELIX 06-iterate → 01-frame loop is intentional. Cycle exemption
728	is declared at the flow level, with rationale, scoped to a
729	type-pair-and-kind triple:
730	
731	```yaml
732	allowed_cycles:
733	  - from_type: implementation-plan
734	    to_type:   prd
735	    kind:      informs
736	    rationale: |
737	      Iteration learnings re-open framing. Each pass is a new walk
738	      modeled by superseding PRD instances.
739	```
740	
741	Instance-level supersession chains (a runaway 17-deep chain) get a
742	runtime warning at instance-check time; they are not a graph violation.
743	
744	### 3.5 Cross-flow block — `external_edges:`
745	
746	ONE canonical location, ONE direction (source authorizes). See §2.2
747	example. The validator never reads the target flow's graph for
748	the cross edge; it only checks that the target flow is in the
749	active marker (otherwise warn) and that the target id resolves in the
750	target flow's instance index (otherwise warn).
751	
752	This is intentional asymmetry. If `helix-infra` is silent about being
753	informed by `helix:prd`, that's fine — `informs` is advisory. If a
754	future flow needs ENFORCED cross-flow prerequisites (hard
755	`requires`), it must propose a bilateral mechanism; this design does
756	NOT extend `external_edges:` to handle that case.
757	
758	### 3.6 Full worked example
759	
760	See §2.2 for the flow graph; see §2.3 for the matching instance
761	frontmatter; see §4.3 for the validator runs against both.

765	## §4 Validator semantics + CLI
766	
767	### 4.1 One binary, four subcommands
768	
769	`library/scripts/helix_check.py` — single py3-stdlib script, no
770	dependencies. Lives in the library (the monorepo decision: one
771	validator, one CI, no by-copy drift).
772	
773	| Subcommand | Layer | Caller                              |
774	| ---------- | ----- | ----------------------------------- |
775	| `type`     | 1     | library CI                          |
776	| `graph`    | 2     | flow CI                      |
777	| `instance` | 3     | pre-commit hook, skill runtime, repo CI |
778	| `marker`   | 0     | consuming-repo CI (THE entrypoint)  |
779	
780	The `marker` subcommand is the entrypoint a consuming repo runs: it
781	parses `.helix.yml`, dispatches `graph` against each declared
782	flow, and dispatches `instance` against every doc under each
783	scope's `root:`. The other subcommands are individually invocable for
784	library CI and flow CI to gate before publication.
785	
786	### 4.2 CLI signatures
787	
788	```text
789	# (1) Marker mode — consuming-repo entrypoint
790	helix_check.py marker <path-to-.helix.yml>
791	    [--strict]                        # warnings → errors
792	    [--json | --json-out PATH]
793	    [--no-instance]                   # marker+graphs only, skip walking scopes
794	    [--library-root PATH]             # override resolver chain (testing)
795	
796	# (2) Graph mode — flow-repo CI
797	helix_check.py graph <flow-root>
798	    --types PATH [--types PATH ...]   # type catalog roots; repeatable
799	    [--schema PATH]                   # default: <library>/schemas/graph.schema.json
800	    [--strict] [--json | --json-out PATH]
801	    [--explain]                       # print resolved edge table
802	
803	# (3) Instance mode — pre-commit hook + skill runtime
804	helix_check.py instance <doc-or-dir> [<doc-or-dir> ...]
805	    --marker <path-to-.helix.yml>     # required; selects active graphs
806	    [--scope <flow-id>]        # restrict to one active flow
807	    [--cross-methodology-edges allow|warn|deny]   # default: warn
808	    [--staged-only]                   # hook optimization
809	    [--strict] [--json | --json-out PATH]
810	
811	# (4) Type mode — library-repo CI
812	helix_check.py type <types-root>
813	    [--self-test]                     # run packaged fixtures
814	    [--strict] [--json | --json-out PATH]
815	```
816	
817	### 4.3 Exit-code classes
818	
819	| Code | Class | Meaning                                                          |
820	| ---- | ----- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
821	| 0    | -     | clean                                                            |
822	| 1    | I     | instance-level violation (edge target missing, frontmatter bad)  |
823	| 2    | G     | graph-level violation (cycles, missing exit gate, bad type ref)  |
824	| 3    | T     | type-level violation (library meta.yml has `relationships:`, etc) |
825	| 4    | M     | marker-level violation (`.helix.yml` malformed)                  |
826	| 5    | R     | resolver / install error (library not found on disk)             |
827	| 64   | U     | usage error (bad flag)                                           |
828	
829	W-class records (W003, W004, W005, R020 etc.) are warnings that do not
830	raise the exit code under default flags; under `--strict` they
831	upgrade to the matching error class (W003 → T003 contextually,
832	W004 → I050, etc.). The summary block always includes a `W:` count
833	so the JSON consumer can surface warnings even at exit 0.
834	
835	Exit code reflects the HIGHEST violation class encountered. The run is
836	EXHAUSTIVE — every violation reachable from the input is collected and
837	emitted, not just the first. The deterministic-checks memory: one run
838	must surface everything; CI shouldn't need N rounds to drain a backlog.
839	
840	### 4.4 Output format
841	
842	- Human-readable to stderr by default (colorized when TTY).
843	- Structured JSON to stdout with `--json`, or to a file with
844	  `--json-out <path>`.
845	- Each violation is a record:
846	  `{code, layer, severity, instance_id, flow, location, message, hint}`
847	  with codes namespaced `T###` `G###` `I###` `M###` `R###`.
848	
849	```json
850	{
851	  "run_id": "2026-06-04T10:42:11Z",
852	  "subcommand": "marker",
853	  "input": ".helix.yml",
854	  "exit_code": 1,
855	  "summary": {"T":0, "G":1, "I":2, "M":0, "R":0},
856	  "violations": [
857	    {"code":"I101","layer":"instance","severity":"error",
858	     "instance_id":"PRD-001",
859	     "location":"docs/helix/01-frame/PRD-001.md:frontmatter.ddx.links[0]",
860	     "message":"edge target FEAT-099 not found in instance corpus",
861	     "hint":"check the id; nearest matches: FEAT-009, FEAT-019"},
862	    {"code":"G201","layer":"graph","severity":"error",
863	     "flow":"helix",
864	     "location":"product/workflows/graph.yml:edges[7]",
865	     "message":"edge type 'informs' not allowed between 'prd' and 'adr' under active graph",
866	     "hint":"ADR is informed_BY prd; the edge belongs on the ADR side as informs from prd"}
867	  ]
868	}
869	```
870	
871	### 4.5 Where validation runs
872	
873	Instance validation runs at THREE points, all calling the same binary
874	with the same flags:
875	
876	1. **Pre-commit hook** — installed by `just install-hooks` in any repo
877	   with `.helix.yml`. Operates on staged files plus their declared edge
878	   targets (catch rename-without-update). `--staged-only` is a speed
879	   override.
880	2. **Skill runtime** — every `/helix` mode that reads or writes an
881	   artifact calls `helix_check.py instance <path> --marker .helix.yml`
882	   on the artifact it's about to mutate AND on any instance it cites.
883	   Failures surface as setup-gap-style messages, not silent dispatch.
884	3. **Repo CI** — the backstop. Catches `--no-verify` hook bypass.
885	
886	The hook is the fastest feedback; the agent check catches drift the
887	agent introduces; CI is the truth-on-merge gate. All three are the
888	same code path.
889	
890	### 4.6 Three worked error messages
891	
892	**(a) Type-shape error (T-class) — library CI catches a malformed promoted type:**
893	
894	```
895	$ python3 scripts/helix_check.py type types/
896	ERROR T003  library/types/feature-specification/meta.yml
897	  relationships block is present on a library type (forbidden under
898	  linkage-relaxation; relationships belong in graph.yml, not meta.yml).
899	  fix: delete the `relationships:` key; encode the type-pair edges in
900	       the flow's graph.yml under nodes/edges.
901	  hint: see docs/helix/02-design/design-2026-06-04-helix-family-marker-and-linkages.md §2.1
902	exit 3
903	```
904	
905	**(b) Graph error (G-class) — flow CI catches an illegal type-pair edge:**
906	
907	```
908	$ python3 ../library/scripts/helix_check.py graph workflows/ \
909	      --types ../library/types --types workflows/artifacts
910	ERROR G201  product/workflows/graph.yml:42
911	  node `prd` declares informs -> `tech-spike`, but `tech-spike` is not
912	  a declared node in this flow.
913	  active flow: helix
914	  fix: either add a node `tech-spike` to graph.yml, or remove the edge.
915	  hint: did you mean `feature-specification`?
916	
917	ERROR G104  product/workflows/graph.yml:17
918	  type `adr` resolves to library:adr, but graph declares overrides
919	  that re-require an already-required section (additive-only rule).
920	exit 2
921	```
922	
923	**(c) Instance error (I-class) — pre-commit hook on a PR adding PRD-001:**
924	
925	```
926	$ python3 .../helix_check.py instance docs/helix/01-frame/PRD-001.md \
927	      --marker .helix.yml
928	ERROR I101  docs/helix/01-frame/PRD-001.md:frontmatter.ddx.links[0]
929	  PRD-001 declares informs -> FEAT-099, but no instance with id FEAT-099
930	  was found under any declared scope.
931	  scopes searched: docs/helix/ (helix)
932	  nearest ids: FEAT-009, FEAT-019, FEAT-013
933	  fix: correct the id, or create the missing FEAT-099 first.
934	
935	ERROR G201  docs/helix/01-frame/PRD-001.md:frontmatter.ddx.links[1]
936	  PRD-001 declares informs -> ADR-002, but the active flow graph
937	  (helix) forbids `informs` between `prd` and `adr`.
938	  allowed edges from prd: informs->feature-specification, informs->user-stories,
939	                          informs->principles, informs->test-plan
940	  fix: ADRs are informed_BY the prd; the edge belongs on ADR-002 as
941	       informs from prd, not on PRD-001.
942	
943	WARN  I003  docs/helix/01-frame/PRD-001.md:frontmatter.ddx.links[2]
944	  edge to FEAT-013 has no `kind:` — defaulting to `informs` per graph.
945	  hint: explicit `kind:` recommended in strict mode.
946	exit 1
947	```
948	
949	All three errors come from ONE run. No short-circuit.
950	
951	### 4.7 Performance contract + instance index cache
952	
953	The marker / instance walk re-reads every doc under each scope's
954	`root:` on each invocation. With three invocation points (hook,
955	skill, CI) and 5000+ instance corpora possible, the naive walk is the
956	ergonomic risk: a slow pre-commit gets `--no-verify`-d.
957	
958	**Budget.** Marker-mode wall-clock budget on a 2024-class laptop
959	(stdlib py3, no native deps):
960	
961	| Corpus size | Budget |
962	| ----------- | ------ |
963	| ≤ 100 docs  | 2s     |
964	| ≤ 1,000 docs | 5s    |
965	| ≤ 10,000 docs | 30s  |
966	| > 10,000     | emits R-class warning **R020** ("consider --cache; perf budget exceeded") |
967	
968	When R020 fires the validator recommends `.helix/index.json` cache
969	mode but completes the run.
970	
971	**Cache mode** (`--cache .helix/index.json`, or `--cache` shorthand
972	defaulting to that path):
973	
974	- The validator persists `{doc_path, mtime_ns, ddx.id, edges_hash}`
975	  for every successfully-parsed instance.
976	- On subsequent runs the validator `stat()`s each doc; only docs
977	  whose mtime_ns > cache re-parse. Renames are detected by missing
978	  path + unchanged id; deletes by missing path + missing id.
979	- `.helix/index.json` is a derived artifact (`.gitignore`-d by the
980	  hook installer); corruption forces a full re-walk on next run, not
981	  a hard failure. Cache parity is asserted by a checksum over
982	  `sorted([(doc_path, ddx.id, edges_hash)])` that the validator emits
983	  with `--json`.
984	- The pre-commit hook defaults to cache mode; CI defaults to no-cache
985	  for fully-deterministic results.
986	
987	**Incremental mode** (`--changed-only <git-ref>`): the validator
988	asks git for the diff against `<git-ref>` and limits the walk to
989	changed docs plus their declared edge targets (catch
990	rename-without-update). Designed for `pre-push` hooks against
991	upstream/main.

995	## §5 Couples with prior design (what changes, what stays)
996	
997	### 5.1 Simplified
998	
999	- **§7.5 co-activation precedence** is largely subsumed. Rules 3
1000	  (single-signal-wins) and 4 (alphabetical tie-break) are deleted; the
1001	  marker IS the operator's intent. Rules 1, 2, 5 survive as a fallback
1002	  block when `.helix.yml` is absent.
1003	- **Per-flow heuristic detection** (file globs, prompt
1004	  patterns) is FROZEN. New flows do not add heuristics; they
1005	  add marker support.
1006	- **Validator drift mode** for instance-level checks is closed: the
1007	  validator that interprets the marker is the same binary that
1008	  validates types and graphs and instances; one upgrade surface.

1011	
1012	- **`relationships:` in `library/types/*/meta.yml`** is removed.

1016	  graph are dropped; the validator computes inverse views.
1017	- **Prior §6.1.1 edge-kind table** (three kinds) is extended to five
1018	  (adds `contains`, `supersedes`); the three original kinds keep their
1019	  semantics.

1030	  cross-cutting`.
1031	- **`allowed_cycles:`** (§6.1.2) extended (per type-pair-with-kind),

1036	### 5.4 Migration
1037	
1038	A one-shot script (`library/scripts/migrate_relationships_to_links.py`)
1039	walks the current `helix` and `helix-infra` repos and:
1040	
1041	1. Strips `relationships:` from every `library/types/*/meta.yml` and
1042	   collects the removed edges.
1043	2. Folds the collected edges into the appropriate flow's
1044	   `graph.yml` `edges:` list with `required: false` (humans tighten
1045	   later).
1046	3. For each instance with body-prose ID citations (FEAT/ADR "Related"
1047	   cells, helix-infra "Paired with" prose, frontmatter `depends_on`
1048	   lists), proposes `ddx.links:` entries as a PR.
1049	4. Adds a `.helix.yml` skeleton at the repo root.
1050	
1051	The migration warns (not errors) on instances lacking `ddx.links:` for
1052	the first cycle; the validator's instance-mode flag
1053	`--require-links false` is the default during migration and flips to
1054	true after the deprecation window.
1055	
1056	**Transition phase matrix (resolves S7 review item).** The relaxation
1057	ships in two named library versions with explicit validator-contract
1058	behavior at each phase. `helix_version:` in the marker is the operator
1059	opt-in to strict-from-day-one.
1060	
1061	| Phase | Library version | Marker `helix_version:` | Library `meta.yml relationships:` | Instance lacks `ddx.links:` | `--require-links` default |
1062	| ----- | --------------- | ----------------------- | --------------------------------- | --------------------------- | ------------------------- |
1063	| A     | 1.0.0           | 1                       | T-warning (W003), not error       | W-warning (W004)            | false                     |
1064	| A     | 1.0.0           | 2                       | T-error (T003)                    | I-error (I050)              | true                      |
1065	| B     | 1.1.0 (~30 days after A) | 1              | T-error (T003)                    | W-warning (W004)            | false                     |
1066	| B     | 1.1.0           | 2                       | T-error (T003)                    | I-error (I050)              | true                      |
1067	| C     | 1.2.0 (≥60 days after A) | any            | T-error                           | I-error (I050)              | true                      |
1068	
1069	Phase A ships the validator and migration script; the library
1070	publishes type strips in a migration PR but does not break consumer
1071	repos that still have legacy frontmatter. Phase B is the cutover for
1072	the LIBRARY: in-tree types must be clean. Phase C is the cutover for
1073	INSTANCES: every consumer must have run the migration or pinned
1074	explicitly. Operators who want strict-mode from day one set
1075	`helix_version: 2` in the marker; the validator then enforces Phase B
1076	+ C behavior regardless of library version.
1077	
1078	The migration script ships with a `--dry-run` mode (default) that
1079	prints proposed edits without writing; CI in consumer repos uses
1080	`--dry-run --require-clean` to fail the build if migration would
1081	edit any file (signal that the migration PR has not landed yet).
1082	
1083	---
1084	
1085	## §6 Tradeoffs + open questions
1086	
1087	### 6.1 Tradeoffs accepted
1088	
1089	- **Marker hard-fails on malformed shape, soft-fails on unknown
1090	  flow id.** Asymmetric on purpose: the marker exists to
1091	  eliminate silent misroutes, so a parse error must be loud; a
1092	  missing-plugin id is recoverable and shouldn't black-hole the repo.
1093	- **Heuristic fallback keeps two activation paths.** Zero migration
1094	  friction for existing installs; cost is the legacy heuristic path
1095	  must be kept tested. The path is FROZEN — no growth — so the cost is
1096	  bounded.
1097	- **Cross-flow edges authorized only by source.** Asymmetric;
1098	  the target may be surprised by inbound `informs`. Acceptable because
1099	  `informs` is advisory-forward; if/when ENFORCED cross-flow
1100	  edges are needed, a separate bilateral mechanism gets designed.
1101	- **Edge kinds CLOSED at five.** New semantics require a validator
1102	  contract bump. `x-vendor-kind` escape hatch keeps experimentation
1103	  cheap.
1104	- **Instance edges resolved by id, not file path.** Bakes a stable id
1105	  scheme into HELIX. Existing instances already have stable
1106	  `ddx.id` values; helix-infra instances (no frontmatter today) must
1107	  acquire `ddx.id` as part of the migration.
1108	- **`contains:` and `supersedes:` are graph-level edge kinds AND
1109	  instance-level annotations.** Slightly redundant for cases where
1110	  supersession is always intra-type, but symmetric with other edges and
1111	  makes traceability rendering uniform.
1112	- **Pre-commit hook is the executable-spec gate.** Determined `--no-verify`
1113	  users bypass; mitigated by CI being the backstop.
1114	- **Validator is one binary with four subcommands** (not four scripts):
1115	  shared resolver, single exit-code contract, harder to drift. Cost:
1116	  more surface in one file; py3-stdlib-only discipline must hold harder.
1117	- **`referenced_by` / `informed_by` dropped from on-disk graphs.**
1118	  Readers grep'ing those keys break; replaced by validator-computed
1119	  inverse views in JSON output.
1120	
1121	### 6.2 Open questions
1122	
1123	- **Cross-flow `requires` (enforced, not advisory)** — the only
1124	  cross-flow edges this design handles are `informs`. A real
1125	  enforced prerequisite across flows (e.g. infra
1126	  apply-evidence MUST precede a product release-notes) needs a
1127	  bilateral mechanism; deferred to whichever flow first asks.
1128	- **Skill-time strictness** — does instance-mode at skill-invocation
1129	  BLOCK on warnings or only on errors? `--strict` is the safest reading
1130	  of the verification-exit-gate memory but raises the floor for every
1131	  existing doc with soft frontmatter. Lean: warn for the deprecation
1132	  cycle, then flip to error.
1133	- **Nested markers** — a polyglot monorepo where one subtree wants its
1134	  own family declaration distinct from the parent. Deferred to v2; v1
1135	  emits warning M010 for any stray nested marker (§1.1) so the case is
1136	  visible. Schema reserves a `parent:` field for v2 override semantics.
1137	- **Pre-commit scope** — staged-only vs staged-plus-edge-targets.
1138	  Targets-too catches rename-without-update; staged-only is faster.
1139	  Default: targets-too with `--staged-only` flag.
1140	- **Instance corpus indexing** — RESOLVED in §4.7: walk by default,
1141	  `.helix/index.json` cache opt-in via `--cache` (hook default),
1142	  R020 warning when corpus exceeds the budget.
1143	- **Structured fix suggestions** — should `hint:` be parseable
1144	  `{action, from, to}` data the skill can auto-apply, or only prose?
1145	  Lean prose; auto-apply risks the skill silently rewriting frontmatter
1146	  incorrectly.
1147	- **`intra-document` containment edges in frontmatter** — overlap with
1148	  body anchors. May collapse to body-only in v2 if traceability tooling
1149	  doesn't need them.
1150	- **`supersedes:` as a flow-level edge kind vs instance-only**
1151	  — currently both. Could collapse to instance-only if no flow
1152	  needs to forbid supersession for a given type.
1153	- **`helix_version:` field** — semver-tracked schema version for the
1154	  marker; `validator_contract:` in graph.yml; `library_type_version:`
1155	  in instances. Three version fields, three coupling questions. v1
1156	  treats them as independent and additive; v2 may consolidate.

The agent authors from general knowledge, ignoring the marker. v1 and v2 both prove the marker / linkage architecture is sound when the skill engages; both fail to prove the skill engages reliably. This plan addresses three coupled gaps: a behavioural test corpus, the skill-engagement levers themselves, and an autonomy slider that controls how aggressively the engaged skill proceeds.

1. **The conversation bench** — a corpus of utterances + expected behaviour the family validates against. Replaces the ad-hoc Bucket A probe set with a growable library.

3. **Cascading flow logic** — the graph used at *runtime* (not just validation): when user asks for type X, the skill consults `graph.yml` for prerequisites and either authors them or offers to.

# Architecture

HELIX is a methodology and artifact catalog for AI-assisted software teams. It
ships portable content and a single routing skill. Architecturally it is three
things:

1. An **artifact catalog** in `workflows/` — templates, prompts, metadata, and
   examples for every artifact type across the seven HELIX activities.
2. A **routing skill** in `skills/helix/SKILL.md` — the single agent-facing
   surface that reads and writes the catalog's artifact instances.
3. A **methodology specification** in `workflows/` — the artifact
   authority hierarchy, activity conventions, artifact-type schema,
   principles, flow contract, and workflow-mode contracts, expressed in
   runtime-neutral terms.

HELIX does not provide a CLI, tracker, queue, or execution loop. Those are

They invoke their agent runtime, and the runtime invokes the HELIX routing skill. ## Vocabulary Contract HELIX uses the following terms in the architecture and in the routing skill. Keeping them separate prevents the marker, graph, catalog, and runtime boundaries from collapsing into one overloaded "methodology" concept. | Term | Meaning | |------|---------| | methodology | The portable HELIX content library: artifact catalog, graph, templates, prompts, workflow-mode contracts, and runtime-neutral rules. | | flow | One active application of a methodology to a bounded project scope. A repo can run multiple flows at once, such as the core product flow and a microsite flow. | | scope instance | The concrete root declared for one flow in `.helix.yml`, such as `docs/helix/` or `website/`. It bounds where the routing skill may read and write for that flow. | | artifact instance | One project document authored from a catalog artifact type, carrying `ddx.id` and optional graph metadata in frontmatter. | | domain lane | A domain-shaped flow specialization, such as product, web, infra, or data. A lane can use the same HELIX methodology while selecting different concerns and handoff rules. | | workflow mode | A behavior inside the public `helix` skill, such as `frame`, `align`, `validate`, `evolve`, `design`, `polish`, or `review`. Modes are not separate public skills. | | public skill | `skills/helix/SKILL.md`, the single operator-facing entry point that resolves the active flow, then dispatches to a workflow mode. | The marker file governs active scope. In the current marker schema, `helix_version: 2` uses `flows:` as the canonical key: ```yaml helix_version: 2 flows: - id: helix instance: product root: docs/helix/ - id: helix instance: microsite root: website/ defaults: flow: helix:product ``` `methodologies:` is the legacy marker key. Parsers may accept it for migration compatibility, but new markers use `flows:` because a methodology may be applied through more than one active flow. The generated catalog graph intentionally still uses a singular `methodology:` key. That key identifies the library that graph nodes come from, not an active project scope. Graph nodes use `library:` for catalog-owned artifact types and may use `local:` for project-local types. Cross-flow edges live in `external_edges:` and must be mirrored by instance frontmatter with `cross_flow: true`; `cross_methodology: true` is a legacy alias accepted only for migration. Colon-qualified IDs carry this same distinction: - `library:prd` means the PRD artifact type from the HELIX catalog. - `local:launch-checklist` means a project-local artifact type. - `helix:product:PRD-001` means an artifact instance in the `helix` product flow. - `helix:microsite:DESIGN-001` means an artifact instance in the `helix` microsite flow. Within a flow, ordinary `ddx.depends_on` links are same-scope links. Cross-scope links connect two instances of the same methodology in different roots. Cross-lane links connect different domain lanes, such as product to infra. It defines every artifact type a HELIX-governed project may produce, organized by the seven HELIX activities: | Activity | Activity slug | Typical artifact types | |----------|-----------|------------------------| | Discover | `00-discover` | Product vision, opportunity brief, constraints | | Frame | `01-frame` | PRD, feature specs, user stories, risks | | Design | `02-design` | Architecture, ADRs, solution designs, technical designs, contracts | | Test | `03-test` | Test plans, test strategies, acceptance criteria | | Build | `04-build` | Implementation plans, execution documents | | Deploy | `05-deploy` | Release plans, operations runbooks, rollout docs | | Iterate | `06-iterate` | Alignment reviews, metrics, retrospectives | Each artifact type in the catalog provides four files: | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `meta.yml` | Artifact-type metadata per the artifact schema | | `template.md` | Markdown skeleton for new instances | | `prompt.md` | Authoring guidance for agents or humans | | `example.md` | Canonical illustrative instance | The full `meta.yml` schema — required fields, recommended fields, validation entries, id-format, dependency declarations, and extension sections — is specified in [`workflows/artifact-schema.md`](../../../workflows/artifact-schema.md). ### Authority hierarchy Every artifact type declares its position in the artifact authority hierarchy. Higher-level artifacts govern lower-level ones; conflicts resolve upward: ``` product vision └─ PRD └─ feature specs / user stories └─ architecture · ADRs └─ solution designs · technical designs └─ test plans └─ implementation plans · code ``` The routing skill's `evolve` workflow mode threads changes downward through this order; `align` audits consistency across it. The authority hierarchy is a HELIX invariant; runtimes execute against it but do not redefine it. ### Artifact instance frontmatter Project artifact instances carry YAML frontmatter under the `ddx:` key. The namespace is historical (DDx is the reference consumer); it does not mean artifacts require DDx. Minimal instance frontmatter: ```yaml --- ddx: id: FEAT-001 type: feature-spec depends_on: - helix.prd status: draft --- ``` `ddx.id` is the only required field. `ddx.depends_on` builds the traceability graph the routing skill traverses inside the active flow. Cross-flow relationships use the marker and graph contract described above. Full field definitions are in [`workflows/artifact-schema.md`](../../../workflows/artifact-schema.md). ## Routing Skill `skills/helix/SKILL.md` is the single agent-facing surface. Any runtime that loads the skill exposes the same capability set. ### What the skill reads and writes | Reads | Writes | |-------|--------| | Artifact catalog (`workflows/activities/…`) — type definitions, templates, prompts | Project artifact instances (`docs/helix/…`) — new or updated content | | Project artifact instances — current state of the dependency graph | Alignment reports, work-item descriptions, design documents | | Methodology docs (`workflows/`) — principles, concerns, activity contracts | Follow-up work descriptions (runtime surfaces these as tracker items, GitHub issues, or markdown stubs) | The skill never writes to a tracker, queue, or evidence store. Those are runtime responsibilities. ### Workflow Modes | Mode | Purpose | |------|---------| | `input` | Convert rough intent into governed work | | `frame` | Create or refine vision, PRD, feature specs, user stories | | `align` | Identify drift, gaps, and contradictions across the artifact graph | | `validate` | Check one artifact instance against its type template and prompt | | `evolve` | Thread a changed requirement through the authority hierarchy | | `design` | Author a technical design before implementation | | `backfill` | Reconstruct missing artifacts from evidence | | `review` | Fresh-eyes review of plans, PRs, or recent work | | `polish` | Refine work items for execution readiness | | `check` / `next` | Decide the safe next action when intent is ambiguous | | `build` / `run` | Bounded implementation pass or operator loop (delegates to runtime) | | `commit` | Commit verified work with traceable message | | `release` | Cut a HELIX content release | ### Skill composability The routing skill reads the artifact catalog when a workflow mode needs a template, prompt, or quality rule; it does not bundle catalog content inside the skill body. This means: - Any runtime that installs the HELIX package exposes the same workflow modes. - Catalog updates (new artifact types, revised templates) take effect without changing the skill body. - The skill body contains zero runtime-specific commands (PRD R-4). Per-runtime packaging notes live in `docs/install/.md`. The skill's normative behavior is self-contained. Runtimes may surface additional affordances through their own packaging layers, such as work-item authoring, queue execution, evidence capture, or prose checking. Those extensions are runtime behavior, not HELIX specification state, and they do not belong in the skill body. ## Artifact Schema as Runtime Contract [`workflows/artifact-schema.md`](../../../workflows/artifact-schema.md) is the contract that lets any compliant runtime register and consume HELIX artifact types and artifact instances. A runtime claiming HELIX compatibility must: 1. Read `meta.yml` for artifact-type metadata. 2. Resolve `ddx.id` and `ddx.depends_on` in instance frontmatter for graph traversal. 3. Treat `workflows/artifact-schema.md` as the schema authority — not its own internal documentation. 4. Preserve unknown fields rather than stripping them. The schema is intentionally open. Runtimes may add extension fields under `ddx:` for operational state, but those fields must be ignorable by other runtimes without changing artifact meaning. Specification fields state artifact intent, relationships, and constraints; runtime operational fields record execution details for that runtime. Minimum runtime primitives required to run the routing skill: 1. Read markdown files from the project filesystem. 2. Write markdown files to the project filesystem. 3. Search files by path or pattern. Shell execution is optional. A runtime satisfying only items 1-3 can run every HELIX workflow mode that does not involve direct code execution. ## Scripts, Runtime Behavior, And Specifications HELIX's product surface is documents plus the public routing skill. Repository scripts are ancillary helpers for maintaining or packaging that content. They may generate reference pages, publish dogfood artifacts into the microsite, or validate catalog consistency, but they are not an operator-facing HELIX CLI and they do not define methodology behavior. Runtime behavior belongs to the runtime. A runtime may provide a tracker, queue, execution loop, evidence store, sandbox, model router, or installer. HELIX can describe the artifact handoff those runtime features must honor, but the runtime owns the concrete mechanism. Specifications capture intended behavior and constraints. They do not track whether a particular script, package, or runtime adapter has already caught up to that intent. Gaps between intent and current code are tracked as work items, review findings, or release notes outside the specification body. ## Packaging HELIX ships as three distribution packages around the same source content (PRD R-7). The source in `workflows/` and `skills/helix/` is never forked. | Package | Target runtime | Format | Install | |---------|---------------|--------|---------| | DDx plugin | DDx | DDx plugin manifest + catalog layout under `.ddx/plugins/helix/` | `ddx install helix` | | Claude Code skill | Claude Code | Skill frontmatter + symlinked content under `.claude/skills/` | Copy or symlink | | Databricks Genie skill | Databricks Genie | Genie skill descriptor + bundled catalog | Genie skill loader | Per-runtime packaging notes — install paths, invocation details, DDx-specific bead conventions, Genie-specific descriptor fields — live in `docs/install/`. None of that detail appears in the routing skill body or the artifact catalog. The adapter boundary between HELIX portable content and DDx-specific surfaces is specified in [CONTRACT-003](contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-adapter-boundary.md). DDx is one reference runtime, not the architecture center. ## Documentation Projection HELIX maintains two complementary documentation trees: | Tree | Role | |------|------| | `workflows/` | Methodology specification — artifact-type schema, activity contracts, principles, concerns, alignment guidance. This is the normative content. | | `docs/helix/` | Dogfood — HELIX's own governing artifacts, authored from HELIX templates. The dogfood is itself subject to `align` workflow-mode runs. | `workflows/` is what adopters install; `docs/helix/` demonstrates the methodology applied to HELIX's own development. The Hugo microsite (when generated) is a read-only projection of both trees, not a source of truth. Methodology invariants — principles, ratchets, activity contracts — are maintained in `workflows/principles.md` and `workflows/ratchets.md`. Before relying on either for design decisions, verify the current state in the file; both are in active flux. ## DDx as One Reference Runtime DDx is the reference runtime for HELIX's own development. HELIX uses DDx for bead tracking, ddx work dispatch, and execution evidence on its own work items. This is HELIX-using-DDx, not HELIX-coupled-to-DDx — the same relationship any adopter has with their chosen runtime. The DDx adapter boundary is defined in [CONTRACT-003](contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-adapter-boundary.md). In brief: - HELIX provides to DDx: artifact catalog, routing skill, artifact-type schema. - DDx provides to HELIX (as runtime): bead tracker, ddx work, evidence store, plugin packaging, prose checker. - Neither side owns the other's internals. CONTRACT-003 also catalogs known boundary leaks — places in the current codebase where runtime-specific language has crept into HELIX portable content — and describes the resolution for each. ## Quality Attributes | Attribute | Strategy | |-----------|---------| | Runtime portability | Skill body and catalog contain zero runtime-specific commands; portability check on every release (PRD R-4) | | Authority-hierarchy coherence | `align` mode audits consistency; `evolve` mode propagates change from the highest-authority artifact down | | Catalog completeness | Seven-activity coverage; each type has template, prompt, meta, example | | Self-application | `docs/helix/` is authored from HELIX templates; `align` workflow-mode runs catch dogfood drift (PRD R-6) | | Schema openness | Consumers add extension fields; unknown fields are preserved, not stripped | | Distribution breadth | Three packaging targets (DDx, Claude Code, Genie); source never forked (PRD R-7) | ## References - [Product Vision](../00-discover/product-vision.md) - [PRD](../01-frame/prd.md) — especially R-4 (runtime-neutral), R-5 (methodology spec), R-7 (packaging) - [CONTRACT-003: DDx Adapter Boundary](contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-adapter-boundary.md) — the boundary between HELIX and DDx - [Artifact Schema](../../../workflows/artifact-schema.md) — normative schema for `meta.yml` and `ddx:` frontmatter - [Routing Skill](../../../skills/helix/SKILL.md) — the single agent-facing surface - [workflows/principles.md](../../../workflows/principles.md) - [workflows/ratchets.md](../../../workflows/ratchets.md)
